question: Does the model correctly identify the note as a whole note (represented by an open oval shape without a stem)?
Does the model correctly describe the position of the note as being below the staff (e.g., associated with a ledger line)?
Does the model correctly identify that there is exactly one note shown in the image, rather than a chord or melody?
Does the model avoid claiming the presence of accidentals (sharps, flats), key signatures, or time signatures that are not in the image?
Does the model use logical deduction to determine the note's pitch based on its vertical position relative to the clef's reference line?
Does the response explicitly select one of the provided options (A, B, C, or D)?
